characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Over the course of the series, he finishes in 1st place four times, and both 2nd place and 3rd place twice, giving him a total of 8 points byNASCARscoring rules and the 9th place in the racers by the end of the series. The Alleged Car: The Turbo Terrific seems to fall to pieces at least once per episode. Alliterative Name:PeterPerfect. Butt-Monkey: He regularly suffers particularly embarrassing accidents or mechanical failures, second only to Dick Dastardly. Catchphrase: Usually "Oh, fiddlesticks!" whenever something goes wrong.

characters.name: Peter Perfect (Driver of the Turbo Terrific)
characters.description: Over the course of the series, he finishes in 1st place four times, and both 2nd place and 3rd place twice, giving him a total of 8 points byNASCARscoring rules and the 9th place in the racers by the end of the series. The Alleged Car: The Turbo Terrific seems to fall to pieces at least once per episode. Alliterative Name:PeterPerfect. Butt-Monkey: He regularly suffers particularly embarrassing accidents or mechanical failures, second only to Dick Dastardly. Catchphrase: Usually "Oh, fiddlesticks!" whenever something goes wrong.

characters.name: Rufus Ruffcut & Sawtooth (Drivers of the Buzzwagon)
characters.description: ByNASCARscoring rules, he does incredibly well through consistently placing in the top 3 finishing spots; with three 1st place finishes,six2nd place finishes, and four 3rd place finishes, he ends up scoring 13 points and thus officially would be considered the 2nd place winner by the end of the series. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Sawtooth wears a yellow helmet, a yellow scarf, goggles, and yellow gloves. Alliterative Name:RufusRuffcut. Bamboo Technology: The Buzz Wagon appears to basically be a kart made out of wood with saws for wheels. Big Guy, Little Guy: Rufus and Sawtooth, respectively.

characters.name: Sergeant Blast & Private Meekley (Drivers of the Army Surplus Special)
characters.description: They have one of the most abysmal scoring runs across the course of the series; they do come in 1st place three times, and in 2nd place just the once, but those are the only times they place in the top three in the entirety of the Wacky Races. ByNASCARrules, they thus end up ranking in 10th place by the series end, with a meagre 4 points. Abnormal Ammo: The Surplus Special can shoot different types of ammo, such as bubble gum to stop other racers. Drill Sergeant Nasty: Blast is loud, aggressive, and usually patronizing to Meekley. Dub Name Change:Blast is Sargento Bombarda in Portuguese, Sergent Grosse-Pomme in France, and Gunsō Kakka (軍曹閣下) in Japan.Meekley is Soldat Petit-Pois in France and Shinpei-kun (新兵くん) in Japan.The Mexican dub changed the car's name toSuper Chatarra Especial(Super Junk Special); it counts asCultural Translation, because army surplus is not a thing in Latin America. Blast is Sargento Bombarda in Portuguese, Sergent Grosse-Pomme in France, and Gunsō Kakka (軍曹閣下) in Japan.

characters.name: The Ant Hill Mob (Drivers of the Bulletproof Bomb)
characters.description: A group of seven pint-sized 1920s-style gangsters composing of Clyde, their leader, Ring-A-Ding, Rug Bug Benny, Mac, Danny, Kirby and Willy who race in the Bulletproof Bomb. They are constantly preoccupied with getting caught by the police and are able to use "getaway power", which involves all members, except for Clyde, extending their legs through the bottom of the car and running. ByNASCARracing rules, the Ant Hill Mob manages to score a respectable 11 points and secure themselves 4th place in the final tally of racers, with four 1st place finishes, five 2nd place finishes, and two 3rd place finishes. Bank Robbery: When Dastardly uses a police siren to trick the other racers into stopping, Clyde tells his companions to remember their "alibi" for a bank robbery. Boring, but Practical: Their car is the second most normal out of all the racers after the Turbo Terrific, but they've clinched more high-place finishers than several of the wackier wacky race cars. In fact, out of the four racers who have came in first four times, they have the most second-place victories. Characterization Marches On: In the first episode, they were set up as secondary antagonists after Dick Dastardly, being just as willing to cheat as he was and significantly better at it than him. While they still feared the law over the rest of the series, in subsequent episodes they're just another racing team, and become outright heroes inThe Perils of Penelope Pitstop.

characters.name: Prof. Pat Pending (Driver of the Convert-a-Car)
characters.description: Pat Pending scores three 1st place finishes, two 2nd place finishes, and five 3rd place finishes over the entire series. Per theNASCARrules, that leaves him with 10 points for a 7th place position. Alliterative Name:ProfessorPatPending. Dub Name Change: His name in Spanish is Professor Locovich, with 'Loco' meaning 'Insane'. Dudley Do-Right Stops to Help: He frequently stops and uses his car's inventions to help the other racers, or uses it to deal with whatever trap Dastardly has set up. Unlike Peter Perfect, whom also constantly invokes this trope, Pending rarely ends up troubled or inconvenienced from doing so. Foil: One of two to Dick Dastardly, the other being Peter Perfect. In Pat's case, he goes out of his way to foil Dick Dastardly's attempts at cheating, and while he uses his brains just as much as Dastardly allhisways of doing so are perfectly legal because they're usually part of his car.

characters.name: Penelope Pitstop (Driver of the Compact Pussycat)
characters.description: Penelope has a very swingy record over the course of the Wacky Races; she comes in at 1st place four times, 2nd place twice, and 3rd place five times, but often comes in at 4th place or lower. ByNASCARscoring, she ends up solidly in the middle of the racer rankings at 5th place. Action Girl: In "Idaho A-Go-Go", when lured by Dastardly into a Red Riding Hood trap, Dastardly has Muttley dress as the Wolf, and, as Grandma, asks her to hide in the closet. But she answers the door and gives everyone who answers, including Peter Perfect, the oldFrying Pan of Doom. Affectionate Nickname: She is called "Pretty Penny" for short, especially by Peter Perfect. Alliterative Name:PenelopePitstop. Breakout Character: Like Dastardly and Muttley, Penelope is popular enough to get her ownspin-off series.

characters.name: The Slag Brothers (Drivers of the Bouldermobile)
characters.description: Incredibly, the Slagmobile manages to consistently place in the top 3 finishing spots across the entire Wacky Races the most of any of the contestants; scoring three 1st placeand3rd place finishes, they also achieve a whoppingeight2nd place finishes. ByNASCARrules, that tallies up to 14 points in total, the single highest score of any Wacky Racer... That's right; these guys would officially end the series with the grand prize as the 1st place racing car! Bamboo Technology: Their car is a rock. Don't ask how it works, it just does. Contemporary Caveman: Hairy guys in loincloths, in a stone car. The Dividual: An extreme example considering they're twins who lookandact exactly the same so trying to figure out who's Rock and who's Gravel is ultimately pointless. Dub Name Change: Known as Pietro and Rocco in the Spanish-language dub, which also translated their surname to Macana (a Latin American term for the clubs they wield).

characters.name: The Gruesome Twosome (Drivers of the Creepy Coupe)
characters.description: ByNASCARscoring rules, they officially would be considered the 3rd place winner by the end of the series, with three 1st and 2nd place finishes and six 3rd place finishers, for a total of 12 points. Bigger on the Inside: The small belfry of their car houses a gigantic dragon and sea serpent. Big Guy, Little Guy: Big Gruesome and Little Gruesome, obviously. Captain Ersatz: Of Frankenstein and Dracula, of course. Dark Is Not Evil: Despite their macabre nature, they're actually among the racers who are more likely to help their competitors out of trouble. Justified in that the Creepy Coupe is behind only the Convert-a-Car and the Mean Machine in terms of resources; Pat Pending will usually help everyone else with gusto and ease, whereas Dick Dastardly... well, won't.

characters.name: The Red Max (Driver of the Crimson Haybaler)
characters.description: Over the course of the series, he finishes in both 1st place and 3rd place three times each, as well as finishing in 2nd place four times, giving him a total of 10 points byNASCARscoring rules and the 6th place in the racers by the end of the series. Ace Pilot: Max's appearance and vehicle are themed on World War I fighter pilots and post-WWI barnstormers, and uses a lot of aviation slang and terminology in his dialogue. Butt-Monkey: Possibly the third worst example after Dick Dastardly (who usually brings it on himself) and Peter Perfect, as the Haybailer's limited flight capabilities make it prime fodder for Michael Maltese'sGravity Is a Harsh Mistressgags. Dub Name Change: Known as Baron Hans Fritz in the Spanish-language dub. The Crimson Haybaler was renamed as the Stuka Rakuda. Family-Friendly Firearms: In the Dreamcast/PS2game, the Haybailer's machine gun actually fires paintballs, and the car can also directly launch its propeller as a homing projectile.

characters.name: Lazy Luke & Blubber Bear (Drivers of the Arkansas Chuggabug)
characters.description: Over the course of the series, he finishes in both 1st place and 3rd place four times each, as well as 2nd place just once, giving him a total of 9 points byNASCARscoring rules and the 9th place in the racers by the end of the series. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Blubber wears a blue helmet with goggles and a green scarf. Alliterative Name:LazyLuke.BlubberBear. Bamboo Technology: Not quite as bad as the Slag Brothers or Rufus Ruffcut, but their car is powered by a steam boiler made out of an old-fashioned potbellied stove. One episode reveals that their engine is assisted by a squirrel on an exercise wheel. Beary Funny: Blubber may be a bear, but he's hardly scary.

characters.name: Dick Dastardly & Muttley (Drivers of the Mean Machine)
characters.description: Of course, as the series' designated villain, byNASCARrules, Dick Dastardly finishes the Wacky Races ranked in atdead last, with 0 points; he never gets a single 1st, 2nd or 3rd place finish in the series. In fact, he almost always finishes dead last in the race, and if he doesn't finish dead last,somethingwill happen to disqualify him. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Muttley wears a red collar. Alliterative Name:DickDastardly. Angrish: When not snickering, Muttley's dialogue mainly consists of muttered grumbling such as "Sassarassafrassa..." Breakout Character: Dick Dastardly and Muttley are easily two ofWacky Races' most notable characters, enough that the two gottheir own series, a recurring villain role inYogi's Treasure HuntandYo Yogi!, and they are the main villains ofThe Funtastic World of Hanna-BarberaandSCOOB!. The two also almost always show up in revival efforts or similar Hanna-Barbera racing series (with one of the outliers beingLaff-A-Lympics, which couldn't use Dastardly due to legal issues). They even appear in a Japanese-made video-pachinko machine known as "Kenken's Hawaii DE Aloha", a Wacky Races-themed game that features Dastardly and Muttley vacationing in Hawaii.
